title: LicenseTag
description: Per-asset license metadata for SoapBox content-posture governance (AA2-3). Every ingested media/document asset carries one of these tags; the license-router (tools/soapbox/license-router.mjs, AA2-4) is a pure function from this tag to a serving posture (HOST | EMBED | AGGREGATE | REJECT). See design/soapbox/content-posture-spec.md and design/soapbox/melek-content-tiers.md.

license.description: The specific license identifier as published by the source (e.g. 'CC-BY-4.0', 'CC0-1.0', 'PD-US-expired', 'US-Gov-Work', 'YouTube-Standard-License', 'All-Rights-Reserved', 'unknown'). Free-form to preserve provenance; licenseFamily is the normalized form the router reads.

licenseFamily.description: Normalized license family the router branches on. PD/CC0/CC-BY/CC-BY-SA/gov/user-original are free-to-host (=> HOST). CC-NC is NonCommercial and NOT host-eligible for a commercial platform (=> AGGREGATE). copyrighted-3p routes by sourceLicensed (EMBED if the source is licensed, else REJECT). unknown is never hosted (=> AGGREGATE).

copyrightStatus.description: Coarse copyright status, independent of the precise license. Advisory/audit field; the router decides from licenseFamily + sourceLicensed, not this. 'open-licensed' = under an open license (CC/etc.) but still copyrighted.

source.description: Where the asset (or its official player) comes from: a host identifier or origin (e.g. 'wikimedia.org', 'youtube.com', 'archive.org', 'user-upload', a scraper host). For copyrighted-3p this is the embed/player host the router checks against the official-player whitelist.

sourceLicensed.description: THE decisive flag for copyrighted-3p assets: is the source we would put in front of the user itself licensed to deliver this content? true = an official licensed player (YouTube/Vimeo/Dailymotion/3Speak official embed) => EMBED. false = an unlicensed/scraper source => REJECT. For free-to-host families this is ignored by the router (we serve the bytes ourselves).

tier.description: OPTIONAL cached routing result. The license-router is the source of truth; this field, if present, should equal router(tag). Stored only to avoid recomputation, never trusted over the router.

attribution.description: Attribution string that MUST be displayed for CC-BY / CC-BY-SA (and any license requiring credit). Carried through to every HOST surface.

flags.description: Independent rights signals that do NOT change the license posture but mark the asset for a separate right-of-publicity / trademark review before promotion on a HOST surface (a recognizable person, a brand/logo, a missing model release, a trademark).

provenance.description: Provenance chain for the transparency score: how this tag was derived (fetched-from URL, license field as reported, who/what asserted it, timestamps). Free-form; carried for audit.

fetchedFrom.description: Canonical URL the asset/metadata was fetched from.

assertedBy.description: Adapter / ingestor / user that asserted this license.

confidence.description: Confidence (0..1) that licenseFamily/sourceLicensed are correct. Low-confidence copyrighted/unknown assets should be treated conservatively (AGGREGATE/REJECT) by downstream policy.
